---
title: STIG Compliance Checker (Reserved Slot) — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: stig-compliance
adapter-class: conformance
mission-class: policy
status: reserved
phase: phase-planning
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-04-14'
aspirational: true
---

# STIG Compliance Checker (Reserved Slot) — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `reserved` · **Class:** `conformance` · **Mission:** `policy` · **Phase:** `phase-planning`

**Canon source:** `canon/adapter-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The STIG Compliance Adapter is the **DISA STIG evaluation edge** of the UIAO substrate. As a conformance-class policy adapter, it ingests XCCDF assessment results from SCAP engines (OpenSCAP, SCAP Workbench, STIG Viewer automation, or OPA-based STIG rule engines) and normalizes them into canonical claims that map STIG rule outcomes to NIST 800-53 controls.

STIG compliance is operationally distinct from generic vulnerability scanning ([`vuln-scan`](../vuln-scan/vuln-scan.html)). STIGs are configuration-hardening checks against a specific DISA-published baseline for a specific platform (RHEL 8 STIG, Windows Server 2022 STIG, Apache 2.4 STIG, etc.). Each rule has a stable identifier (`SV-NNNNNN`), a severity (CAT I / II / III), and a defined pass / fail / not-applicable result. The adapter is the canonical bridge between that DISA-native shape and the substrate's claim model.

Three primary functions:

1. **XCCDF result parsing.** Parses the structured XCCDF (Extensible Configuration Checklist Description Format) output produced by SCAP-compliant scanners, extracting rule identifier, severity, result, title, and remediation reference per rule into canonical claim shape.
2. **Benchmark-scoped assessment summary.** Aggregates per-benchmark statistics — pass count, fail count, not-applicable count, percent compliant — keyed to the specific STIG benchmark version and the asset under assessment. The summary is the input to FedRAMP continuous-monitoring evidence and to baseline-drift findings.
3. **STIG-to-NIST control mapping.** Translates STIG rule outcomes into NIST 800-53 control evidence via the canonical STIG-to-NIST cross-walk (CCI mappings). A failed STIG rule becomes a per-control deficiency record that flows into the POA&M generation pipeline.

**Implementation:** `src/uiao/adapters/stigcompliance_adapter.py`. Conformance: 30/30 PASS. SCAP-engine driver selection is tracked as ODA-14; activation requires a per-adapter ADR.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `xccdf-results`, `stig-benchmark-summaries`, `stig-to-nist-cci-mappings`

**Reads:** SCAP / XCCDF assessment results (JSON or XML) with `rule_id`, `severity` (CAT I / II / III), `result` (pass / fail / notapplicable / error / unknown), `title`, and remediation reference per rule. Read-only — the adapter never invokes the SCAP engine; the scan runs on its own schedule and this adapter parses what it produces.

**Emits:** `findings.json` (per-rule claim records with rule id, severity, result, title, asset, benchmark version), `metadata.json` (per-benchmark summary with pass / fail / not-applicable counts and percent-compliant), plus `EvidenceObject` records that feed the CM-6, CM-7, and CA-7 control evidence stream.

**Does NOT:** execute SCAP scans, modify system configuration, remediate findings, or apply STIG-recommended changes. Read-only policy evaluation; remediation belongs to the configuration-management workflow.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `CM-6`, `CM-7`, `CA-7`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **CM-6** Configuration Settings | Primary | STIG rule evaluation verifies configuration settings against DISA-published baselines per platform — the canonical evidence for configuration hardening compliance. |
| **CM-7** Least Functionality | Supporting | STIG rules include least-functionality checks (disabled services, removed packages, restricted features); failures surface as least-functionality findings. |
| **CA-7** Continuous Monitoring | Supporting | Scheduled STIG assessment provides continuous compliance posture evidence with the freshness-window semantics defined in canon. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `TBD` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `TBD` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `interval` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

Slot reserved per ARCHITECTURE.md §3.5 and §13 open decision ODA-14. Candidate engines include SCAP / OpenSCAP, STIG Viewer automation, or OPA-based STIG rule engines. Selection deferred to Phase 2 planning.

Advisory mapping (per ADR-043 D4 / UIAO_132 §5, pending FedRAMP RFC-0026 ratification): satisfies requirement RV5-CA07-VLN (OS / DB / web application configuration scan) on the traditional FedRAMP authorization pathway, with the modernized pathway gated on publication of the VDR Balance Improvement Release.

Pairs with [`vuln-scan`](../vuln-scan/vuln-scan.html), [`siem`](../siem/siem.html), and [`patch-state`](../patch-state/patch-state.html) as the four-adapter compliance-telemetry quartet that produces the continuous-monitoring evidence pack.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-025 — Adapter activation framework
- ADR-043 — FedRAMP RFC-0026 pathway alignment
- UIAO_132 — Continuous Monitoring Pack
- https://github.com/FedRAMP/community/discussions/130

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
